q: What do you prefer doing on a day off?
opts: Working out or pushing myself physically
Eating something good and relaxing without a care
Staying quiet — resting or reading
Exploring something new and unexpected

q: How do you show someone you care about them?
opts: Through concrete actions, not words
By saying it openly and being affectionate
I struggle to express it, but when I do, it's everything
Through teasing and flirting — but genuinely

q: What kind of humor do you prefer?
opts: Dark, ironic or absurd
Spontaneous and physical — laughing until it hurts
Clever and subtle, wordplay and wit
I'm not much of a joker — I prefer sincerity

q: What's your relationship with order and rules?
opts: I love structure — I can't function without it
Rules are made to be broken
I prioritize efficiency above all else
Rules suffocate me — I need freedom

q: What do you value most in a relationship?
opts: Absolute honesty and directness
Patience and mutual understanding
Intelligence and someone who challenges me
Loyalty and constant presence

q: What type of music do you prefer?
opts: Metal, heavy rock or intense music
Classical or elegant instrumental music
Underground pop, indie or dark music
I don't have a strong preference

q: How do you react in a dangerous situation?
opts: I throw myself in without thinking twice
I analyze and act with precision
I protect others before myself
I prefer to avoid conflict whenever possible

q: What would you order at a restaurant?
opts: Something simple and comforting (rice, burgers, ramen)
Street food or something from a popular local spot
An elegant, well-presented dish
Whatever dessert or sweet catches my eye

q: How do you prefer to communicate with someone?
opts: Few words — let actions do the talking
Open and direct, holding nothing back
Through hints and subtle games
In a deep and thoughtful way

q: What is your biggest fear?
opts: Someone I care about getting hurt
Complete boredom — nothing left to excite me
Losing control of an important situation
Someone getting to know me too well

q: If you could spend unlimited money in one day, what would you use it for?
opts: Top-of-the-line training or combat equipment
An epic feast with the best food in the world
Rare books, experimental tech or unique artifacts
Nothing special — I'd save it

q: What's your social style?
opts: I'm the life of the party — I love socializing
Loyal to my inner circle, but I don't open up easily
Reserved but interesting once you get to know me
Shy or observant — it takes a lot to get to know me

q: What do you find most attractive in someone?
opts: That they're strong and not intimidated by me
That they're intelligent and always surprise me
That they're kind and patient with everyone
That they're honest and don't beat around the bush

q: What would your role be in a team?
opts: The leader who makes the calls
The silent specialist who works in the shadows
The one who looks after and supports everyone
Living chaos — improvise and win

q: What do you think about the supernatural and mysterious?
opts: I'm fascinated — I'd love to dive in headfirst
I find it interesting as a concept
It doesn't particularly interest me
It earns my respect, but I'd rather stay away

q: How do you react to failure?
opts: I get back up and try again immediately
I process it in silence and learn from it
It affects me deeply — I worry about disappointing others
It doesn't faze me if I did what I had to do

q: What environment makes you feel most comfortable?
opts: Dark, quiet and a little mysterious
Busy and full of energy
Home or a familiar, cozy space
An orderly and efficient space

q: How important is work in your life?
opts: It's my reason for being — I'm fully dedicated
I love it, but I know how to balance it
I work to live, I don't live to work
Work is exciting when it truly drives me

q: What kind of people drive you crazy?
opts: Cowards or people who don't put in effort
People who are too loud or unpredictable
Those who lie or manipulate without remorse
Boring and utterly predictable people

q: How do you react when someone disappoints you?
opts: I say it to their face, no sugarcoating
I keep it to myself, but I never forget
I forgive easily — I'm not one to hold grudges
It depends on who it is and how much they matter to me

q: What would a perfect first date look like for you?
opts: A competition or physical activity — something with action
Elegant dinner, great atmosphere and deep conversation
Something dark and mysterious: a horror film or a strange place
Something simple and unpretentious — good food and good talk

q: How long does it take you to open up to someone new?
opts: A long time — I'm very guarded
It depends on who they are; I assess as I go
Gradually, over time
I'm very open from the very first moment

q: How important is loyalty to you?
opts: It's everything — without it, there's nothing
It's fundamental, but it has to be earned first
Loyalty comes with affection — it can't be demanded
I believe in individual freedom above all else

q: What kind of movie would you choose to watch?
opts: Action, fights and explosions
Horror, mystery or the supernatural
Deep and emotional drama
Comedy or something light and relaxing

q: How do you handle your emotional life?
opts: Completely privately — nobody knows
I share it only with very close people
I'm fairly open emotionally
I blend humor with the emotional stuff

q: How important are tradition and respect to you?
opts: They're central to my life
I respect them but they don't define me
I take them as a guide, not a law
Traditions feel limiting to me

q: What type of challenge do you prefer?
opts: Physical: combat, endurance, strength
Intellectual: strategy, chess, puzzles
Emotional or creative: art, music, writing
Pure improvisation in the middle of chaos

q: What phrase from someone would affect you the most?
opts: "You're not good enough"
"You're too intense and unpredictable"
"I can't trust you"
"You're too closed off from others"

q: What phrase best describes your attitude toward life?
opts: "Perfection is the bare minimum"
"Live fast, never look back"
"The unknown is the most interesting thing"
"The people I love are my reason for everything"
